titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Christopher Felver, Photographic Portraits of Native American Authors
Felver (Christopher) , Photographic Portraits of Native American Authors
titlestmt.author: by Ashley Cale and Matthew Daniel Mason

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 16349559.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday March 2, 2023 at 04:05

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: Photographic portraits of Native American poets and writers created by Christopher Felver for his book, Tending the Fire: Native Voices and Portraits (University of New Mexico Press, 2017). The collection includes ninety-seven photographs that appear in the work as well as twenty portraits not published in it.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from George Robert Minkoff Inc. on the Walter McClintock Memorial Fund, 2022.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Portraits Published in Tending the Fire , 1995-2015. II. Portraits Not Published in Tending the Fire , 1996-2016.

bioghist.head: Christopher Felver (born 1946)
bioghist.p: Christopher Felver is an American photographer and filmmaker.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Christopher Felver, Photographic Portraits of Native American Authors. Yale Collection of Western Americana, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.

The description of this guide includes the page portraits appeared in Christopher Felver, Tending the Fire: Native Voices and Portraits (Albuquerque: University of New Mexico Press, 2017) and the site of the portrait when referenced in the work or in information available in January 2023 from the website of the Getty Images photography agency.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic portraits of Native American poets and writers created by Christopher Felver for his book, Tending the Fire: Native Voices and Portraits (University of New Mexico Press, 2017). The collection includes ninety-seven photographs that appear in the work as well as twenty portraits not published in it.
Titled, signed and dated by the photographer in graphite on versos of photographic prints.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Christopher Felver, Photographic Portraits of Native American Authors, is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

did.unittitle: Portraits Published in Tending the Fire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic portraits of Native American authors created by Christopher Felver and published in his book, Tending the Fire: Native Voices and Portraits (Albuquerque: University of New Mexico Press, 2017). The description of this series includes the page the portrait appeared in the work and the site of the portrait when referenced in the work or in information available in January 2023 from the website of the Getty Images photography agency.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by surname of the subject.

did.unittitle: Portraits Not Published in Tending the Fire

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographic portraits of Native American authors created by Christopher Felver and not published in his book, Tending the Fire: Native Voices and Portraits (Albuquerque: University of New Mexico Press, 2017). The description of this series includes the site of the portrait when referenced by a variant image published in the work as well as sites and date corrections based on information available in January 2023 from the Getty Images photography agency website.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by surname of the subject.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Born Florence Anthony and also known as Pelorhankhe Ai Ogawa; photographed at Los Angeles, California; misdated 2011 on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Likely photographed at Albuquerque, New Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographed at San Francisco, California; misdated 2012 on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Also known as Papa Coke; father of Allison Adelle Hedge Coke and grandfather of Travis Coke; photographed at Oklahoma City, Oklahoma

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographed at Santa Fe, New Mexico; misdated 2015 on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Misidentified as Sydney Freelove; photographed at Los Angeles, California; misdated 2015 on verso

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographed at San Francisco, California; misdated 2012 on verso
